extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won stage 7 sprint after recovering from crash outside Brussels finish, edging EWAN in final meters with Jumbo-Visma leadout fractured.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma leadout abandoned GROENEWEGEN early when Marcus Burkhart took front, forcing solo positioning in final kilometer.
SAGAN's Bora positioned perfectly with LASS leading, then inexplicably SAGAN didn't follow, got boxed and had to wait for gaps.
VIVIANI executed textbook positioning inside top-10 entering final kilometer but lacked final 100m speed against pure fastmen.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: Demonstrated mental resilience winning after Brussels crash, plus raw speed to hold off EWAN despite compromised leadout
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Nearly won despite starting sprint from worse position than SAGAN/GROENEWEGEN, suggesting superior peak wattage in final 150m
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Positioned perfectly on LASS wheel but failed to follow teammate's acceleration, suggesting tactical hesitation or mistimed jump
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Face injury from road sign 7km into stage suggests vulnerability to close-quarters positioning in nervous pelotons
